title: Ruagea mollicoma
intro: Rauvolfia mollicoma, commonly known as Ruagea mollicoma, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of hypertension, anxiety, and insomnia, due to its ability to lower blood pressure and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Ruagea mollicoma include alkaloids such as yohimbine and raubasine, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Traditional herbal preparations made from Ruagea mollicoma include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
taxonomy: Ruagea mollicoma is classified in the family Guttiferae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family is also known as the Clusiaceae family.
